title: Quality Calibration Review / WU-13 Handoff
we_started_believing: We might still need another planning loop around the quality calibration memo before any new build work.
we_ended_believing: The memo was useful, but continuity already reflects the accepted calibration state. The next agent should begin WU-13, with manual live smoke still recommended before first external users.
transformation: Validated the calibration memo against the live code, recorded the caveats, and normalized the handoff so the next session can start WU-13 immediately without reopening Sprint 4 debate.

title: WU-13 Account & Dashboard
we_started_believing: WU-13 still mostly existed as a spec and would need broad front-end work before it could be considered real.
we_ended_believing: WU-13 is implemented and locally validated end to end. The remaining gap is external human auth delivery verification, not missing application code.
transformation: Built auth helpers, account routes, login/signup/callback/dashboard pages, API key generation, and audit ownership wiring under test; then proved the owned-account path with a disposable Supabase user and DB-level verification before stopping at the inbox/GitHub validation boundary.

title: Auth Verification + Live Quality Validation
we_started_believing: The remaining blocker after WU-13 was probably just a human inbox or GitHub login session.
we_ended_believing: Magic-link auth is fully verified in local and production environments. The real next blocker is live quality/robustness: over-reporting on the dogfood site and stalled runs on mainstream non-Next.js sites. The only auth gap left is a real credentialed GitHub login.
transformation: Verified the callback flow with generated Supabase links, fixed hash-token callback handling, realigned Vercel Supabase env vars after JWT mismatches, confirmed GitHub OAuth reaches the real login page, and then discovered that live audit quality/robustness has become the next highest-priority problem.
